calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing For Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Oct-26-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Pamela J. Herzig, Esq. (415) 495-0112 for Petitioner and Moving Party Matthews
M. Rebeca Mendoza, Deputy Attorney General (510) 622-2211 for Respondent and Opposing Party Department of Motor Vehicles
RULING:
A R G U E D; PETITIONER BARBARA MATTHEWS' Petition For Writ Of Mandate: The matter is remanded to the Department of Motor Vehicles for further hearing to allow the arresting officers to testify as to the identity of the individual tested and whether the testing was properly done. Attorney Mendoza shall submit a proposed form of order. (301/AJR/DHS-X) Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR #8620

Notice Of Hearing For Petition Of Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Sep-20-2004 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

RULING:
There being no appearances the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PETITIONER BARBARA MATTHEWS' Petition Of Writ Of Mandate: DENIED for failure to comply with CCP 1005(b) requiring the moving papers to be filed AND served at least 21 days before the hearing. Honorable James L. Warren. Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558 (301)

Notice Of Hearing Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jun-29-2004 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
RULING:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PETITIONER BARBARA MATTHEWS' Petition For Writ Of Mandate: DENIED; insufficient service. Petitioner mailed notice of the amended writ on 06/09/04, only 20 days before the hearing. Per CCP 1005(b), Petitioner must mail notice and supporting papers 26 days before the hearing. (301/JLW/) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
